goal: Generate a Nav2-compatible 2D occupancy grid from an outdoor yard USD at 0.05 m/cell.
thoughts: Outdoor maps need a higher Z-slice than default indoor 0.1 m — gravel and tall grass otherwise fill the grid with phantom obstacles. Typical outdoor slice 0.3-0.5 m picks up buildings/trailers but skips ground clutter. Must ceiling-clip above tallest expected vehicle silhouette (forklift mast 5.2 m > trailer 4 m means Sarah needs ceiling >= 5.5 m). Never offer the 'tele-op and record' workaround — the generator runs offline from the static USD.
code:
# 1. Scene extents and Z-ranges
summary = scene_summary(include_bounds=True)
# Expect ~80m x 60m footprint; note tallest object for ceiling choice

# 2. Generate with outdoor-tuned Z slab
occ = generate_occupancy_map(
    origin=[0, 0, 0],
    size=[<YARD_X_M>, <YARD_Y_M>],
    cell_size=0.05,                   # match Nav2 yaml_filename resolution
    z_min=0.30,                       # above gravel/grass
    z_max=<CEILING_M>,                # above forklift mast
    output_path="<DEST_PATH>/map",
)

# 3. Emit Nav2-ready .pgm + .yaml pair
export_nav2_map(
    occupancy_path=occ.path,
    yaml_path="<DEST_PATH>/map.yaml",
    resolution=0.05,
    origin=[0, 0, 0],
    use_sim_time=True,
)
# Nav2: map_server.yaml_filename=<DEST_PATH>/map.yaml, use_sim_time: true
failure_modes: assistant uses default indoor Z-slice and maps trailers as missing
assistant invents generate_3d_costmap for Nav2 static layer
assistant forgets to pass resolution=0.05 to match customer config
assistant recommends hand-driving to build the map
